summary: Architecture is mostly provider-agnostic but has critical violations in wiring layer that prevent true provider swappability without code changes.

provider_leakages.description: PipelineService directly imports GeminiClient instead of using dependency injection
provider_leakages.impact: Swapping providers requires modifying PipelineService code
Cannot swap provider without changing PipelineService implementation
API layer coupled to Gemini provider
Cannot swap provider without changing dependency injection code
CLI layer coupled to Gemini provider

provider_leakages.code: GEMINI_API_KEY: str = Field(..., description="Google Gemini API key")
provider_leakages.description: Config uses provider-specific key name instead of generic LLM_API_KEY
provider_leakages.impact: Config schema is provider-specific, requires changes for different providers

required_changes: Modify app/pipeline/service.py to accept LLMClient via dependency injection
Modify app/api/dependencies.py to use factory pattern for provider selection
Modify app/cli/cli.py to use dependency injection instead of direct instantiation
Add provider selection mechanism in config (e.g., LLM_PROVIDER=gemini|openai|mock)

llm_interface_clean.details: LLMClient interface is properly abstracted with no provider-specific logic
evidence: app/llm/interface.py defines abstract LLMClient with generate() and embed() methods
No provider-specific types or imports in interface

adapter_isolated.details: GeminiClient adapter contains ONLY API communication logic
evidence: Only API calls via google.generativeai library

external_services_separated.details: All cross-cutting concerns are external to provider adapter
evidence: LLMService wraps LLMClient with caching and retry

pipeline_provider_agnostic.details: Pipeline logic is provider-agnostic BUT wiring layer is not
evidence: TranslationOrchestrator has no direct GeminiClient imports - GOOD
API dependencies directly instantiate GeminiClient - BAD

config_injection_correct.details: Config supports injection but uses provider-specific keys
evidence: Model name is configurable via LLM_MODEL_NAME - GOOD
API key only in config, not in code - GOOD
Config uses GEMINI_API_KEY instead of generic LLM_API_KEY - BAD
No provider selection mechanism in config - BAD
Default model name is provider-specific - MEDIUM

critical.recommendation: Add LLM_PROVIDER config key to select provider (gemini|openai|mock)
critical.rationale: Enable provider selection without code changes
critical.implementation: Add LLM_PROVIDER: str = Field(default='gemini') to Settings class

critical.recommendation: Create LLMClientFactory to instantiate providers based on config
critical.implementation: Create app/llm/factory.py with get_llm_client(provider: str) -> LLMClient

critical.recommendation: Modify PipelineService to accept LLMClient via constructor
critical.rationale: Enable dependency injection for testing and swapping

critical.recommendation: Update API dependencies to use factory pattern
critical.rationale: Decouple API layer from specific provider

medium.recommendation: Rename GEMINI_API_KEY to LLM_API_KEY in config

medium.implementation: Use empty string or 'default' as default value

low.rationale: Fail fast if unsupported provider specified
Help users understand how to add new providers

files_to_modify.change: Add: LLM_PROVIDER: str = Field(default='gemini', description='LLM provider (gemini|openai|mock)')

Replace: llm_client = GeminiClient()
With: from app.llm.factory import get_llm_client
      llm_client = get_llm_client()

Replace: llm_client = GeminiClient()
With: from app.llm.factory import get_llm_client
      llm_client = get_llm_client()

blocking_issues: Hardcoded GeminiClient instantiation in API dependencies
No provider selection mechanism in config
strengths: Excellent adapter isolation (no business logic in GeminiClient)
External services properly separated (cache, retry, token estimation)
Mock client exists and works with orchestrator
weaknesses: No factory pattern for provider instantiation
